data.name: AccessPropertyExplicitlySetTitle
data.value: Every object needs to specify a value for the Access property

data.name: AccessPropertyExplicitlySetMessageFormat
data.value: {0} '{1}' does not explicitly have the Access property set.

data.name: AccessPropertyExplicitlySetDescription
data.value: Every AL object should explicitly specify its Access property to make its intended accessibility clear. Explicitly setting Access prevents accidental exposure or over-restriction of objects and forces conscious design decisions about visibility.

data.name: ApplicationAreaOnApiPageTitle
data.value: The 'ApplicationArea' property is not applicable to API pages.

data.name: ApplicationAreaOnApiPageMessageFormat
data.value: The 'ApplicationArea' property is not applicable to API pages and should be removed.

data.name: ApplicationAreaOnApiPageDescription
data.value: The ApplicationArea property has no effect on API pages. API pages are consumed programmatically and do not participate in the user interface personalization model. Keeping this property on API pages adds noise without functional value and should be removed to keep the code clean.

data.name: ApplicationAreaOnApiPageCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Remove 'ApplicationArea' property

data.name: AutoCalcFieldsOnlyOnFlowFieldsTitle
data.value: Avoids a runtime error from using AutoCalcFields on a field that is not a FlowField or a field of type Blob.

data.name: AutoCalcFieldsOnlyOnFlowFieldsMessageFormat
data.value: The AutoCalcFields method should only be used with FlowFields or fields of type Blob. The field {0} is not a FlowField or of type Blob.

data.name: AutoCalcFieldsOnlyOnFlowFieldsDescription
data.value: AutoCalcFields should only be used for FlowFields or Blob fields

data.name: AutoIncrementInTemporaryTableTitle
data.value: AutoIncrement fields are not supported in temporary tables

data.name: AutoIncrementInTemporaryTableMessageFormat
data.value: AutoIncrement is used in a table with TableType = Temporary, which will cause a runtime error. Remove AutoIncrement or make the table non-temporary.

data.name: AutoIncrementInTemporaryTableDescription
data.value: AutoIncrement relies on SQL Server to generate the next value when inserting records. Temporary tables are only in-memory in Business Central and are not created on SQL Server, so SQL Server cannot generate AutoIncrement values. This results in runtime failures when code inserts into the temporary table.

data.name: ClearCodeunitSingleInstanceTitle
data.value: Clear/ClearAll does not reset SingleInstance codeunit state

data.name: ClearCodeunitSingleInstanceMessageFormat
data.value: Clear(All) does not affect or change values for global variables in the single instance '{0}: Codeunit {1}'.

data.name: ClearCodeunitSingleInstanceDescription
data.value: SingleInstance codeunits keep their global (state) variables for the lifetime of the session. Calling Clear() on a SingleInstance codeunit variable, or calling ClearAll() in an object that contains local/global SingleInstance codeunit variables, does not reset the stored global values inside those SingleInstance codeunits.

data.name: EditableFlowFieldTitle
data.value: FlowFields should not be editable

data.name: EditableFlowFieldMessageFormat
data.value: Field '{0}' is editable, which is uncommon for a FlowField. Set Editable = false, or add a comment to justify why editing is intentional.

data.name: EditableFlowFieldDescription
data.value: FlowFields are calculated fields whose values are derived from other data and are not stored on the record itself. Allowing a FlowField to be editable exposes an input surface that the platform does not process by default. User-entered values are neither validated nor persisted unless custom logic is explicitly implemented.

data.name: EditableFlowFieldCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Set Editable property to False

data.name: EventPublisherIsHandledByVarTitle
data.value: Handled parameters in event signatures should be passed by var

data.name: EventPublisherIsHandledByVarMessageFormat
data.value: Parameter '{0}' must use the 'var' keyword if the publisher parameter is 'var'.

data.name: EventPublisherIsHandledByVarDescription
data.value: Identifies event parameters of type boolean named IsHandled or Handled that are not passed by var.

data.name: EventPublisherIsHandledByVarCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Add var keyword

data.name: EventSubscriberVarKeywordTitle
data.value: Event subscriber var keyword mismatch.

data.name: EventSubscriberVarKeywordMessageFormat
data.value: Parameter '{0}' must use the 'var' keyword if the publisher parameter is 'var'.

data.name: EventSubscriberVarKeywordDescription
data.value: Ensures that event subscriber methods use 'var' keyword for parameters as defined by event publisher.

data.name: EventSubscriberVarKeywordCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Add var keyword

data.name: ExtensiblePropertyExplicitlySetTitle
data.value: Explicitly set Extensible property on public objects

data.name: ExtensiblePropertyExplicitlySetMessageFormat
data.value: The property Extensible should be explicitly set for public objects.

data.name: ExtensiblePropertyExplicitlySetDescription
data.value: Public objects should explicitly define their extensibility by setting the Extensible property to either true or false. Leaving this property unspecified relies on implicit defaults and can lead to avoidable compatibility and maintenance issues.

data.name: ExtensiblePropertyExplicitlySetCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Set Extensible property to platform default

data.name: FilterStringSingleQuoteEscapingTitle
data.value: Invalid single-quote escaping in filter string

data.name: FilterStringSingleQuoteEscapingMessageFormat
data.value: Filter string {0} uses incorrect single-quote escaping for a not-empty filter.

data.name: FilterStringSingleQuoteEscapingDescription
data.value: A filter string representing an is-not-empty condition is incorrectly escaped.

data.name: FilterStringSingleQuoteEscapingCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Fix single-quote escaping in filter string

data.name: FlowFilterFieldAssignmentTitle
data.value: Set values for FlowFilter fields using filtering methods

data.name: FlowFilterFieldAssignmentMessageFormat
data.value: Direct assignment to the '{0}' field of type FlowFilter invalidates the filter logic for calculations. Use .SetFilter() or .SetRange() to set the filter correctly.

data.name: FlowFilterFieldAssignmentDescription
data.value: Directly assigning values to FlowFilter fields bypasses their purpose and invalidates the filtering logic, resulting in incorrect or unintended calculations. Instead, use the .SetFilter() or .SetRange() methods to define the appropriate filters.

data.name: GuidEmptyStringComparisonTitle
data.value: Use IsNullGuid() to check for empty GUID values.

data.name: GuidEmptyStringComparisonMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'IsNullGuid({0})' method instead of comparing the GUID '{0}' with {1}, as this will result in a runtime error.

data.name: GuidEmptyStringComparisonDescription
data.value: When checking if a GUID is empty, avoid using direct comparisons such as MyGuid = '', as this will result in a runtime error due to the invalid GUID format. Instead, use the IsNullGuid() method to perform the check safely and correctly.

data.name: GuidEmptyStringComparisonCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Use IsNullGuid()

data.name: IsHandledParameterAssignmentTitle
data.value: Incorrect 'IsHandled' parameter assignment.

data.name: IsHandledParameterAssignmentMessageFormat
data.value: Do not set the 'IsHandled' parameter to 'false' or any value that may be evaluated as false.

data.name: IsHandledParameterAssignmentDescription
data.value: The 'IsHandled' parameter must always be set to 'true' when handled. Avoid assigning 'false' or any value that may be evaluated as false.

data.name: JsonTokenJPathUsesDoubleQuotesTitle
data.value: Use two single quotes instead of double quotes in JPath expressions

data.name: JsonTokenJPathUsesDoubleQuotesMessageFormat
data.value: Double quote character detected in JPath expression. Replace all double quotes (") with two single quotes ('').

data.name: JsonTokenJPathUsesDoubleQuotesDescription
data.value: Detects and warns against the use of double-quote (") characters in JPath expressions.

data.name: JsonTokenJPathUsesDoubleQuotesCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Replace double quotes (") with two single quotes ('')

data.name: ListObjectsAreOneBasedTitle
data.value: Zero index access on 1-based List objects

data.name: ListObjectsAreOneBasedMessageFormat
data.value: List objects are 1-based

data.name: ListObjectsAreOneBasedDescription
data.value: List objects are 1-based, meaning indexing starts from 1 instead of the conventional 0.

data.name: MandatoryFieldMissingOnApiPageTitle
data.value: Mandatory fields must be exposed on API pages.

data.name: MandatoryFieldMissingOnApiPageMessageFormat
data.value: "Field 'Rec.{0}' exposed with the name '{1}' should always be included on API Pages."

data.name: MandatoryFieldMissingOnApiPageDescription
data.value: API pages must expose a minimal set of mandatory system fields to ensure correct identification, change tracking, and webhook support.

data.name: MandatoryFieldMissingOnApiPageCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Add missing fields

data.name: ODataKeyFieldsShouldUseSystemIdTitle
data.value: The ODataKeyFields property should use the SystemId field.

data.name: ODataKeyFieldsShouldUseSystemIdMessageFormat
data.value: The ODataKeyFields property is set to '{0}', but should use the SystemId field.

data.name: ODataKeyFieldsShouldUseSystemIdDescription
data.value: The ODataKeyFields property defines the unique and stable key for OData exposure. Using SystemId is strongly recommended because it is immutable and guaranteed not to change over the lifetime of a record. Using other fields can lead to unstable OData URLs, broken integrations, and data consistency issues.

data.name: OperatorAndPlaceholderInFilterExpressionTitle
data.value: Avoid unsupported operators with placeholders in SetFilter expressions

data.name: OperatorAndPlaceholderInFilterExpressionMessageFormat
data.value: Found operator '{0}' together with placeholder '{1}' in filter expression, which results in unexpected behavior. Use the StrSubstNo() method to circumvent this.

data.name: OperatorAndPlaceholderInFilterExpressionDescription
data.value: This rule validates filter expressions passed to Record.SetFilter when placeholders (%1, %2, …) are used together with certain operators such as *, @, and ?. When these operators are included directly in the filter string, placeholders are not substituted as expected, leading to incorrect or unpredictable filtering results.

data.name: OperatorAndPlaceholderInFilterExpressionCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Wrap filter expression in StrSubstNo()

data.name: PageRecordArgumentMismatchTitle
data.value: Page record argument does not match source table

data.name: PageRecordArgumentMismatchMessageFormat
data.value: Argument {0}: cannot convert from {1} to {2}.

data.name: PageRecordArgumentMismatchDescription
data.value: This rule validates that record arguments passed to page-related operations match the page's SourceTable.

data.name: PageRecordMethodRequiresSourceTableTitle
data.value: Page record methods require a SourceTable

data.name: PageRecordMethodRequiresSourceTableMessageFormat
data.value: SourceTable property is not defined on Page '{0}'. Method '{1}' requires a SourceTable.

data.name: PageRecordMethodRequiresSourceTableDescription
data.value: Calling these methods on a page without a SourceTable results in invalid or unpredictable runtime behavior, because the page has no table context to operate on.

data.name: RecordGetProcedureArgumentsTitle
data.value: Incorrect number or type of arguments in .Get() method on Record object

data.name: RecordGetProcedureArgumentsMessageFormat
data.value: Invalid arguments in .Get() method for record '{0}': {1}.

data.name: RecordGetProcedureArgumentsDescription
data.value: This rule ensures that calls to the built-in .Get() procedure on Record objects have the correct number and types of arguments matching the primary key (PK) of the record in question.

data.name: PossibleOverflowAssigningTitle
data.value: Possible overflow when assigning text to a smaller target type.

data.name: PossibleOverflowAssigningMessageFormat
data.value: Possible overflow assigning '{0}' to '{1}'.

data.name: PossibleOverflowAssigningDescription
data.value: Reports cases where a Text/Label (or a computed Text such as concatenation) can exceed the maximum length of the target type (e.g., assigning to Code[20], Text[10], or a field with a smaller MaxLength).

data.name: PossibleOverflowAssigningAppendMaxLengthToLabelCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Add MaxLength property to Label declaration

data.name: PossibleOverflowAssigningApplyCopyStrCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Refactor by wrapping with Text.CopyStr(...)

data.name: SetRangeWithFilterOperatorsTitle
data.value: Filter operators should not be used in SetRange

data.name: SetRangeWithFilterOperatorsMessageFormat
data.value: SetRange is called with a filter expression. Use SetFilter instead.

data.name: SetRangeWithFilterOperatorsDescription
data.value: SetRange is intended for simple value-based range filtering. When filter operators such as <>, .., *, &, or | are used, the call represents a filter expression and may lead to unintended behavior. In such cases, SetFilter must be used to express the filter explicitly and correctly.

data.name: SetRangeWithFilterOperatorsCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Replace SetRange with SetFilter

data.name: TableRelationFieldLengthTitle
data.value: Table relation field length mismatch

data.name: TableRelationFieldLengthMessageFormat
data.value: The related field has length {0} ({1}) which is longer than the current field length {2} ({3})

data.name: TableRelationFieldLengthDescription
data.value: The field with table relation should have at least the same length as the referenced field.

data.name: TemporaryRecordTriggerInvocationTitle
data.value: Avoid triggering table logic on temporary records

data.name: TemporaryRecordTriggerInvocationMessageFormat
data.value: Do not execute table triggers or validation methods on temporary record variables.

data.name: TemporaryRecordTriggerInvocationDescription
data.value: Executing table triggers (Insert, Modify, Delete, DeleteAll, ModifyAll) or calling Validate on temporary record variables causes the temporary scope to be lost. This can result in unintended database changes because the record is no longer treated as temporary. Only execute triggers when the table itself is defined as temporary.

data.name: TransferFieldsNameMismatchTitle
data.value: Inconsistent field names across TransferFields

data.name: TransferFieldsNameMismatchMessageFormat
data.value: Field with ID {0} resolves to different field names ({3} ≠ {4}) between TransferFields-coupled tables {1} and {2}.

data.name: TransferFieldsNameMismatchDescription
data.value: The same field ID is used with different field names in tables coupled via TransferFields. While this does not cause a runtime error, it increases the risk of accidental coupling of fields with different intent.

data.name: TransferFieldsTypeMismatchTitle
data.value: Incompatible field types across TransferFields

data.name: TransferFieldsTypeMismatchMessageFormat
data.value: Fields {5} and {6} with ID {0} have a possible incompatible field type ({3} → {4}) between TransferFields-coupled tables {1} and {2}.

data.name: TransferFieldsTypeMismatchDescription
data.value: Tables coupled via TransferFields must define matching field types for the same field ID. A type mismatch will result in a runtime error when TransferFields is executed, as incompatible field types cannot be safely copied.

data.name: UseSequentialGuidTitle
data.value: Use CreateSequentialGuid() instead of CreateGuid() for key fields

data.name: UseSequentialGuidMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'CreateSequentialGuid()' instead of '{0}'. {1}

data.name: UseSequentialGuidDescription
data.value: CreateGuid() generates random GUIDs that cause SQL index fragmentation when inserted into key fields. CreateSequentialGuid() produces partially-sequential GUIDs that reduce index fragmentation by 20-40%, improving insert, read, and update performance. Use CreateSequentialGuid() when the GUID is assigned to a field that is part of a table key.

data.name: UseSequentialGuidCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Replace with CreateSequentialGuid()

data.name: UsePartialRecordsOnReadTitle
data.value: Use partial records on read operation

data.name: UsePartialRecordsOnReadMessageFormat
data.value: Use SetLoadFields before '{0}.{1}()' to improve performance by loading only the fields that are needed.

data.name: UsePartialRecordsOnReadDescription
data.value: When reading records from the database using Get, Find, FindFirst, FindLast, or FindSet, use SetLoadFields to specify which fields to load. This avoids loading all normal fields (including those from table extensions), reducing SQL joins and data transfer. For performance reasons, it is not recommended to use partial records on a record that will do inserts, deletes, renames, field transfers, or copies to temporary records.

data.name: UsePartialRecordsOnReadCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Add SetLoadFields

data.name: PartialRecordsBeforeWriteOperationTitle
data.value: Do not use partial records before write operations

data.name: PartialRecordsBeforeWriteOperationMessageFormat
data.value: Do not use '{0}' before write operations ({1}) on '{2}'. Partial records cause a JIT load on write, resulting in an extra SQL roundtrip and possible runtime errors.

data.name: PartialRecordsBeforeWriteOperationDescription
data.value: When SetLoadFields, AddLoadFields, or SetBaseLoadFields is used on a record that subsequently performs Insert, Modify, Delete, Rename, TransferFields, or Copy, the platform must JIT-load all remaining fields before the write. This causes an extra SQL roundtrip (making the code slower than not using partial records) and can trigger 'Inconsistent read of field(s)' or 'JIT loading of field(s) failed' runtime errors under concurrent access.

data.name: PartialRecordsBeforeWriteOperationCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Remove SetLoadFields

data.name: ReportLayoutPropertyLengthTitle
data.value: Report layout property exceeds maximum length

data.name: ReportLayoutPropertyLengthMessageFormat
data.value: Report layout {0} is {1} characters long, but the maximum is 250. Business Central will fail at runtime when selecting this layout.

data.name: ReportLayoutPropertyLengthDescription
data.value: The Caption and Summary properties on report layout blocks are stored in database fields limited to 250 characters. Values exceeding this limit compile without error but cause a runtime crash ("The length of the string is N, but it must be less than or equal to 250 characters") when a user opens the Report Layout Selection page in Business Central.
